HomiCSx
  • API reference
  • homicsx.utils
  • View page source

homicsx.utils

homicsx.utils.E_nu_to_kappa_mu_3d(E, nu)[source]

Converts Young’s modulus (E) and Poisson’s ratio (nu) to shear modulus (mu) and bulk modulus (kappa).

Parameters:

  • E (float) – Young’s modulus.

  • nu (float) – Poisson’s ratio.

Returns:

Shear modulus. kappa (float): Bulk modulus.

Return type:

mu (float)

homicsx.utils.E_nu_to_kappa_mu_2d_plane_strain(E, nu)[source]

2D plane strain conversion.

homicsx.utils.kappa_mu_to_E_nu_3d(kappa, mu)[source]

Converts bulk modulus (kappa) and shear modulus (mu) to Young’s modulus (E) and Poisson’s ratio (nu).

Parameters:

  • kappa (float) – Bulk modulus.

  • mu (float) – Shear modulus.

Returns:

Young’s modulus. nu (float): Poisson’s ratio.

Return type:

E (float)

homicsx.utils.kappa_mu_to_E_nu_2d_plane_strain(kappa, mu)[source]

2D plane strain conversion.

homicsx.utils.extract_effective_moduli_3d(C_hom)[source]

Extracts effective moduli (kappa_eff, mu_eff, E_eff, nu_eff) from the homogenized stiffness matrix.

homicsx.utils.extract_effective_moduli_2d_plane_strain(C_hom)[source]

Extract effective moduli from 2D homogenized stiffness (plane strain). Voigt order: [xx, yy, xy]

homicsx.utils.mori_tanaka(E_m, nu_m, E_f, nu_f, vol_f, dim=3)[source]

Computes the effective stiffness tensor using the Mori-Tanaka homogenization scheme.

This semi-analytical model accounts for the interaction between inclusions by assuming each inclusion is embedded in the average stress field of the matrix. It is more accurate than the dilute approximation for moderate volume fractions.

Parameters:

  • E_m, nu_m (float) – Young’s modulus and Poisson’s ratio of the matrix.

  • E_f, nu_f (float) – Young’s modulus and Poisson’s ratio of the fiber/inclusion.

  • vol_f (float) – Volume fraction of the inclusions (0.0 to 1.0).

  • dim (int, default 3) – Spatial dimension (2 or 3).

Returns:

The effective (homogenized) stiffness matrix C_eff in Voigt notation.

Return type:

np.ndarray

homicsx.utils.hashin_shtrikman(E_m, nu_m, E_f, nu_f, vol_f, dim=3)[source]

Computes the Hashin-Shtrikman upper and lower bounds for effective properties.

These bounds represent the theoretical limits for any isotropic composite without assuming a specific geometry (like circles or squares). For a stiffer inclusion (E_f > E_m), the ‘lower’ bound corresponds to the matrix-dominated case (Mori-Tanaka) and the ‘upper’ bound to the inclusion-dominated case.

Parameters:

  • E_m, nu_m (float) – Properties of the matrix.

  • E_f, nu_f (float) – Properties of the fiber/inclusion.

  • vol_f (float) – Volume fraction of the inclusion.

  • dim (int, default 3) – Spatial dimension (2 for Plane Strain, 3 for 3D).

Returns:

A dictionary containing tuples of (Lower, Upper) bounds for Bulk modulus (kappa), Shear modulus (mu), Young’s modulus (E), and Poisson’s ratio (nu).

Return type:

dict
